citation_short: FRUS XI, Doc. 18 (Rogers to Nixon, 3 Apr 1971)
title: Memorandum From Secretary of State Rogers to President Nixon
author_or_org: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State
strengths: Near-contemporaneous U.S. diplomatic reporting that preserves the Dacca consul general's aggregate death estimate and identifies major target zones.
limitations: Aggregate all-status citywide deaths only; no direct civilian/military split; relies on early, incomplete reporting.
notes: Corroborates a 4,000-6,000 direct-death range in the Dacca area over the first several days.

title: Telegram 978 From the Consulate General in Dacca to the Department of State
author_or_org: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State
strengths: Near-contemporaneous granular cable with named sub-events, target areas, and one direct survivor-based EPR loss report.
limitations: Some points are hearsay or second-hand; not a full citywide accounting; status splits remain incomplete.
notes: Important for the arson-and-shoot method in old Dacca and for the EPR survivor report of 700 killed.

title: U.S. Consulate (Dacca) Cable, 'Killings at University'
author_or_org: National Security Archive / U.S. Consulate Dacca
strengths: Close-in-time account tied to an American observer and later archival summary; strong on event detail.
limitations: No final citywide total; some observations are indirect; not designed as a full forensic count.
notes: Useful for showing that many university victims were plainly noncombatant while some armed-resistance evidence existed.

citation_short: NSA Document 5, 'Extent of Casualties in Dacca' (31 Mar 1971)
title: U.S. Consulate (Dacca) Cable, 'Extent of Casualties in Dacca'
author_or_org: National Security Archive / U.S. Consulate Dacca
pages_or_sections: pp. 1-2; NSA collection summary lines 134-138; page screenshots reviewed
strengths: Best single near-contemporaneous Dhaka source for total deaths plus sub-claims for police and university victims.
limitations: Still an early aggregate estimate; subcomponents are partial and do not sum to the whole; student status is not fully resolved.
notes: Reports 4,000-6,000 deaths from military action, a conservative 500-1,000 university student deaths, and 600-800 police killed.

citation_short: Rao Farman Ali, A Stranger in My Own Country
title: A Stranger in My Own Country: East Pakistan, 1969-1971
pages_or_sections: p. 76 appendix, 'Allotment of Troops to Tasks'
strengths: Contains a compact operational plan listing West Pakistani units and targeted EPR/police strengths for Dacca.
limitations: Participant memoir with obvious self-serving risks; target strengths conflict with Salik on some counts; not a casualty source.
notes: Critical denominator input, but must be used with caution and triangulation.

title: Witness to Surrender
pages_or_sections: Operation SEARCHLIGHT-I; lines 580-591 and 1683-1684 in web edition
strengths: Detailed operational narrative by a Pakistani officer with explicit Dacca tasking, H-hour timing, and target assignments.
limitations: Participant memoir, not neutral; gives conflicting EPR/police target headcounts relative to Farman; not a systematic casualty tabulation.
notes: Useful for theater description and for the source-disagreement range around organized-armed presence.

citation_short: Sukhwant Singh, 'The blitzkrieg in East Pakistan – I'
title: 1971: The blitzkrieg in East Pakistan – I
strengths: Useful order-of-battle and reinforcement context, including reduced-strength battalion evidence.
limitations: Secondary and reproduced online; not Dhaka-only; battalion-strength points are contextual rather than direct episode counts.
notes: Supports a low-end assumption that some West Pakistani battalions in March 1971 were thin and that about two battalions were airlifted to Dacca in reduced strength.

title: Population of Dhaka City: Past, Present and Future
author_or_org: M.A. Shahidul Islam and A.S.M. Mahbubun Nabi
pages_or_sections: pp. 1-2, Table 1 and discussion of 1961-1974 growth
strengths: Provides transparent city-level historical population series and explicitly warns that post-1971 capital-status growth inflated the 1961-1974 rise.
limitations: No direct 1971 census point; OCR is messy; city-boundary changes matter; requires backcasting for March 1971.
notes: Best available denominator anchor found for Dhaka city population.

citation_short: ICJ, The Events in East Pakistan 1971 (1972)
title: The Events in East Pakistan 1971: A Legal Study
geographic_scope: East Pakistan / Bangladesh, with city-level passages
strengths: Early legal/human-rights synthesis with useful city-level observations and some cross-checkable chronology.
limitations: Not a denominator or incident microdata source; many casualty figures are contextual rather than for direct estimation.
notes: Supports both Dhaka flight modeling and Chittagong episodeization.

title: Dead Reckoning: Memories of the 1971 Bangladesh War
strengths: Directly engages contested witness material and preserves radio-traffic and journalistic excerpts relevant to status ambiguity.
limitations: Highly contested interpretation; should be used narrowly for status-classification caveats, not as a sole numerator source.
notes: Used here only to justify an unknown-status bucket for some university victims.

title: Genocide in Bangladesh
strengths: Compact synthesis of the war's major phases, target groups, displacement, and late-war transition to conventional fighting.
limitations: Conflict-wide and partly advocacy-oriented; not suitable by itself for episode numerators or denominators.
notes: Useful for why a whole-war DR is misleading and why episode splitting is necessary.
